content.full_text:
This letter is in response to your request regarding the miscellaneous provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because your question primarily concerns the
application of a code section not within the Act, we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance.2
Fetn PolrrrcAl- PRACTTcEs Conl,rissroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
April 11,2006
Shirley Fay, Ph.D.
Corning Healthcare District
P.O. Box 996
Coming, CA 96021
RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-06-054
Dear Ms. Fay:
The jurisdiction of the Commission is limited solely to the provisions of the Act.
The Commission has no jurisdiction to advise regarding the application of Government
Code section 1090. You note correctly that there is currently pending legislation to
create a pilot program by which the Commission can advise on section 1090, but this
legislation has yet to be approved by the legislature and currently has no legal effect. The
proposal is described in depth on the Commission's website. Currently, the Attomey
General's office has jurisdiction over that section, although they generally do not provide
advice. Or, you or your spouse may need to retain private counsel if the County of
Tehama does not provide you advice regarding section 1090.
You ask whether the Act prohibits your spouse frorn holding a position on the
board while you are a district manager. The Act does not prohibit you or your spouse
from holding public offices. However, under eertain circumstances, both you and your
spouse may have an obligation to disqualify from decisions pursuant to section 87100 of
the Act. Enclosed please find Can I Vote? Conflicts of Interest Overview and the Ramos
Advice Letter, No. I-00-079.
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3), copy enclosed).


File No. I-06-054
Page No. 2
If you have specific questions about govemmental decisions under the provisions
of the Act you may seek written advice. (See regulation 18329 for the required
information).
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division
Enclosure
JWW:rd
I:\Adviceltrs\06-054

sections.question: You ask whether the Act prohibits your spouse from holding a position on the board while you are a district manager.
sections.conclusion: The Act does not prohibit you or your spouse from holding public offices. However, under certain circumstances, both you and your spouse may have an obligation to disqualify from decisions pursuant to section 87100 of the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Political Reform Act prohibits a spouse from holding a board position while the other spouse serves as a district manager.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not inherently prohibit spouses from holding these positions simultaneously, but both parties may be required to recuse themselves from certain decisions under conflict of interest rules.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice letter (not formal opinion). Also addresses jurisdiction limitation regarding Government Code section 1090, which is outside FPPC's purview. Letter dated April 11, 2006, with File No. I-06-054.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You ask whether the Act prohibits your spouse from holding a position on the board while you are a district manager.

CONCLUSION: The Act does not prohibit you or your spouse from holding public offices. However, under certain circumstances, both you and your spouse may have an obligation to disqualify from decisions pursuant to section 87100 of the Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter responding to a request about whether the Political Reform Act prohibits a spouse from serving on a board while the other spouse is a district manager. The FPPC clarifies that the Act does not prohibit such positions but notes potential recusal obligations under section 87100.
